title: Race-grouped, gap-aware CV as the main anti-leakage filter
summary: Current features are lap-level panel data with strong within-race dependence.
rationale: Current features are lap-level panel data with strong within-race dependence. A row-random or weakly grouped split can overstate AUC because adjacent laps from the same race/strategy regime are near-duplicates. Grouped time-series validation with a gap is a closer match to real deployment and should expose which features truly transfer across races.
implementation_hint: Use `Year_Race` as the primary group. Compare current CV against expanding or rolling `GroupTimeSeriesSplit` with a small lap-gap/embargo near fold boundaries. Re-score candidate features/models only under this stricter protocol and prune anything that collapses.
expected_effect: More honest local CV and better leaderboard stability; may slightly reduce local score while improving generalization.
risk: This may look worse locally at first, and small sample count per race can make fold variance noisy.
